_comment: Generated snapshot of SignalR hub contracts. Update this file whenever a hub method is added, removed, or has its signature changed. Clients (web app, Android) must keep their handlers in sync with this file.

StartPlaybackCommand.description: Start or toggle playback for a playlist item. Handles new playlists, track reorder within an existing playlist, and playlist switches.

parameters.name: trackId
parameters.description: ID of the track to play

GetStateCommand.description: Returns the current MusicPlayerState for the authenticated user, or null if no playback is active.

PlaybackCommand.description: Send a transport command (play, pause, next, previous, seek, shuffle, repeat, volume, mute).

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional payload; type depends on command (e.g. seek position as number, volume as number)

CurrentTimeCommand.description: Report the current playback position from the active client.

ChangeDeviceCommand.description: Transfer playback to a different connected device.

ChangeVolumeCommand.description: Set the volume on the active device and persist the change.

MusicPlayerState.description: Broadcast current player state to all devices belonging to the user.

ConnectedDevicesState.description: Broadcast updated list of connected devices for this user.

parameters.name: devices
parameters.description: All currently connected devices for this user on this hub

ChangeDevice.description: Notify all devices that the active playback device has changed.

SetTime.description: Persist playback progress (time, audio track, subtitle track) for a video item.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Progress payload including videoId, tmdbId, playlistType, playlistId, time, audio, subtitle, subtitleType

RemoveWatched.description: Delete all UserData entries for the specified item, marking it as unwatched.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Progress payload identifying the item to clear

StartPlaybackCommand.description: Start playback for a video item. Creates new player state or resumes/switches playlist.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: Playlist/collection ID (int for movie/tv/collection, Ulid for special)

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Video file ID of the item to start

GetStateCommand.description: Returns the current VideoPlayerState for the authenticated user, or null if no playback is active.

PlaybackCommand.description: Send a transport command to the video player.

ChangeDeviceCommand.description: Transfer video playback to a different connected device.

VideoPlayerState.description: Broadcast current video player state to all devices belonging to the user.

ConnectedDevicesState.description: Broadcast updated list of connected devices for this user.

parameters.name: devices
parameters.description: All currently connected devices for this user on this hub

ChangeDevice.description: Notify all devices that the active playback device has changed.

CastHub._description: Bridges the web app and Chromecast devices. Server methods relay commands to Chromecast; client methods relay Chromecast state back to all connected clients.

GetChromeCasts.description: Returns the names of discovered Chromecast devices on the local network.

SelectChromecast.description: Connect to a specific Chromecast device by name.

Launch.description: Launch the NoMercy receiver application on the connected Chromecast.

CastPlaylist.description: Send a playlist JSON string to the connected Chromecast.

GetMediaStatus.description: Returns the current MediaStatus from the Chromecast.

Play.description: Relay play command to all user clients.

Pause.description: Relay pause command to all user clients.

Ended.description: Relay playback-ended event to all user clients.

GetPlayerState.description: Request the current player state from the cast receiver.

SetNext.description: Relay next-track command to all user clients.

SetPrevious.description: Relay previous-track command to all user clients.

SetPlay.description: Relay play command to all user clients.

SetPause.description: Relay pause command to all user clients.

SetStop.description: Relay stop command to all user clients.

Time.description: Relay time update to all user clients.

Volume.description: Relay volume update to all user clients.

Muted.description: Relay mute state to all user clients.

Item.description: Relay current playlist item update to all user clients.

Playlist.description: Relay full playlist update to all user clients.

SubtitleTracks.description: Relay available subtitle tracks to all user clients.

CurrentSubtitleTrack.description: Relay the active subtitle track to all user clients.

AudioTracks.description: Relay available audio tracks to all user clients.

CurrentAudioTrack.description: Relay the active audio track to all user clients.

PlayerState.description: Relay full cast player state snapshot to all user clients. Broadcasts via 'MusicPlayerState' method name.

SetAudioTrack.description: Command the cast receiver to switch to the specified audio track.

SetSubtitleTrack.description: Command the cast receiver to switch to the specified subtitle track.

SetPlaylistItem.description: Command the cast receiver to jump to a specific playlist item.

SetVolume.description: Command the cast receiver to set volume.

SetMuted.description: Command the cast receiver to set mute state.

SetSeek.description: Command the cast receiver to seek to a position.

GetPlayerState.description: Client should respond with current player state.

SetNext.description: Client should skip to the next item.

SetPrevious.description: Client should skip to the previous item.

MusicPlayerState.description: Client receives full cast player state (Note: method name is 'MusicPlayerState' even for video; this is the cast hub's naming convention).

CurrentSubtitleTrack.description: Client receives the active subtitle track.

CurrentAudioTrack.description: Client receives the active audio track.

SetAudioTrack.description: Client should switch to this audio track.

SetSubtitleTrack.description: Client should switch to this subtitle track.

SetPlaylistItem.description: Client should jump to this playlist item.

SetMuted.description: Client should set its mute state.

SetSeek.description: Client should seek to this position.

DashboardHub._description: Provides server resource monitoring and log streaming for the admin dashboard.

StartResources.description: Start broadcasting CPU/memory/disk resource metrics to connected dashboard clients.

StopResources.description: Stop broadcasting resource metrics when no dashboard clients remain connected.

ConnectedDevicesState.description: Inherited from ConnectionHub — broadcast updated device list to all clients.

RipperHub._description: Provides optical media (Blu-ray/DVD) ripping functionality. Requires moderator role.

GetDriveState.description: Get the current state and metadata of a specific optical drive. Requires moderator role.

parameters.name: drivePath
parameters.description: File system path to the optical drive

ConnectedDevicesState.description: Inherited from ConnectionHub — broadcast updated device list to all clients.

ConnectionHub._description: Base hub inherited by all other hubs. Handles device registration, activity logging, and ConnectedDevicesState broadcasts.
ConnectionHub._note: Not exposed directly as an endpoint. All public methods here are available on every hub that inherits from ConnectionHub.

ConnectedDevicesState.description: Sent to ALL connected clients whenever any client connects or disconnects. Lists devices connected to the same hub endpoint for the same user.

parameters.name: devices
parameters.description: List of currently active Device objects for this user

connectionQueryParameters._description: All hubs inherit from ConnectionHub which reads these query parameters on connect to register the device.

knownIssues.issue: The server sends a 'PlayerState' command but the client method name broadcast is 'MusicPlayerState'. Clients must register a handler for 'MusicPlayerState', not 'PlayerState'.
The 'listId' parameter is typed as 'dynamic' in C#. For movie/tv it is an int, for special it is a Ulid string. Clients must handle both numeric and string values.
The 'specialId' field on VideoProgressRequest is a Ulid (string format). This is inconsistent with 'tmdbId' which is an int.
